name: The remote.exe Binary Used for Application Whitelisting Bypass
description: Detects the use of the `remote.exe` binary to bypass application whitelisting and execute local or remote files. Adversaries can use `remote.exe` to spawn PowerShell sessions, bypass AWL, and execute other commands.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the process creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Windows Security Event ID for Process Creation (4688).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that was executed, which should be 'remote.exe'.

parameters.name: process_path
parameters.description: The full path to the remote.exe executable.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line used to execute remote.exe, including the '/s' switch.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which remote.exe was executed.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
